insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Egypt's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the two breaks they've had, resulting in a slight overall decline in performance with a mean change of -0.050 minutes. When Egypt was pressing before a hydration break, they struggled to maintain their intensity, losing momentum every time and experiencing a significant decline in performance with an average change of -0.200 minutes per minute. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to capitalize on the pause and come back stronger, gaining momentum and seeing an improvement in their performance with an average change of +0.100 minutes per minute. This suggests that Egypt may benefit from adjusting their strategy to take advantage of hydration breaks, particularly when they're not already dominating possession.
